enDef: having or showing a lot of experience of the world and the ability to understand complicated things
example: The system uses a sophisticated algorithm to rank candidates.
exampleCn: 该系统使用一套复杂精妙的算法为候选人排序。

enDef: an opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete information
example: What she said is mere conjecture, not evidence.
exampleCn: 她所说的只是推测，而非证据。

enDef: showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
example: He was meticulous in checking every reference.
exampleCn: 他在核对每条参考文献时一丝不苟。

enDef: recognize or find out something, especially with difficulty
example: We could discern a faint light at the end of the corridor.
exampleCn: 我们能分辨出走廊尽头有一丝微弱的光。

enDef: open to more than one interpretation; not clear in meaning
example: The contract contained several ambiguous clauses.
exampleCn: 合同中有几处条款含糊不清。

enDef: a typical example or pattern of something; a model
example: The study introduced a new paradigm for understanding memory.
exampleCn: 该研究提出了理解记忆的新范式。

enDef: able to recover quickly from difficult conditions
example: Children can be remarkably resilient after hardship.
exampleCn: 儿童在经历困境后往往能展现惊人的韧性。

enDef: an action or event that happens before another more important one and acts as an introduction
example: The conference was merely a prelude to larger reforms.
exampleCn: 这次会议不过是更大规模改革的前奏。

enDef: in accordance with the highest standards; faultless
example: Her attention to detail was impeccable.
exampleCn: 她对细节的关注无可挑剔。

meaning: 无处不在的；普遍存在的
enDef: present, appearing, or found everywhere
example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in everyday life.
exampleCn: 智能手机在日常生活中已无处不在。

enDef: lasting for a very short time
example: Fame in the digital age can be ephemeral.
exampleCn: 数字时代的名声可能转瞬即逝。

enDef: experienced in the imagination through the feelings or actions of another person
example: She took vicarious pleasure in her daughter's success.
exampleCn: 她通过女儿的成功获得间接的满足。

enDef: lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time
example: The novel has become a perennial favorite.
exampleCn: 这部小说成为一本常年畅销的经典。

enDef: make something physically stronger or more solid; combine into a single more effective whole
example: They consolidated their gains before moving forward.
exampleCn: 他们在推进之前先巩固了既得成果。

enDef: a subtle difference in or shade of meaning, expression, or sound
example: She captured every nuance of the argument.
exampleCn: 她捕捉到了该论点的每一个细微差别。

meaning: 回响；回荡；产生广泛影响
enDef: be repeated several times as an echo; have continuing serious effects
example: The scandal reverberated through the entire industry.
exampleCn: 这一丑闻在整个行业中产生了广泛回响。

enDef: fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
example: Her eloquent defense of the thesis impressed the jury.
exampleCn: 她对论文的雄辩辩护令评审印象深刻。

enDef: the quality or fact of being very determined; persistence
example: The job requires tenacity and careful reading.
exampleCn: 这份工作需要坚持和仔细的阅读。

enDef: spread throughout something; diffuse
example: A sense of calm permeated the room.
exampleCn: 一种平静的感觉弥漫在房间里。

enDef: well meaning and kindly
example: A benevolent smile softened her reply.
exampleCn: 一个善意的微笑柔化了她的回应。

enDef: having knowledge or awareness
example: The team was cognizant of the risks involved.
exampleCn: 团队认识到所涉及的风险。

enDef: restrict something within limits; draw a figure around another, touching it but not cutting it
example: The rules circumscribe the scope of the committee.
exampleCn: 规则限制了委员会的职权范围。

enDef: increase rapidly in number; multiply
example: Start-ups proliferated in the early 2000s.
exampleCn: 初创企业在 2000 年代初大量涌现。

enDef: regard with great respect; revere
example: The old professor was venerated by generations of students.
exampleCn: 这位老教授受到一代又一代学生的崇敬。

enDef: make something better or more satisfactory
example: Steps were taken to ameliorate the hardship.
exampleCn: 已采取措施改善困境。

enDef: most noticeable or important
example: The salient points are summarized at the end.
exampleCn: 重点在末尾做了归纳。

enDef: using very few words
example: His laconic reply surprised everyone.
exampleCn: 他言简意赅的回答让所有人都感到意外。

enDef: render obscure, unclear, or unintelligible
example: They were accused of trying to obfuscate the facts.
exampleCn: 他们被指控试图混淆事实。

enDef: examine or inspect closely and thoroughly
example: The committee scrutinized every line of the report.
exampleCn: 委员会仔细审阅了报告的每一行。

enDef: in its original condition; unspoiled; clean and fresh as if new
example: The manuscript was preserved in pristine condition.
exampleCn: 这份手稿保存完好，宛如新制。

enDef: make something less severe, serious, or painful
example: Medication can mitigate the symptoms.
exampleCn: 药物可以缓解症状。

enDef: accept something reluctantly but without protest
example: He acquiesced to their demands without further argument.
exampleCn: 他默许多了他们的要求，不再争辩。

enDef: having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently
example: She articulated her objections with great clarity.
exampleCn: 她非常清晰地表达了自己的反对意见。

enDef: dealing with things sensibly and realistically
example: They took a pragmatic approach to the crisis.
exampleCn: 他们对危机采取了务实的态度。

enDef: kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of
example: She cast a surreptitious glance at her watch.
exampleCn: 她偷偷瞥了一眼手表。

enDef: an illogical or surprising lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts
example: There was a discrepancy between the two reports.
exampleCn: 两份报告之间存在不一致。

enDef: conducive to success; favorable
example: It was an auspicious start to the year.
exampleCn: 这是一年的吉祥开端。

enDef: having an obstinately uncooperative attitude
example: The recalcitrant student refused to follow instructions.
exampleCn: 这位桀骜不驯的学生拒绝听从指示。

enDef: a person who advocates or supports something
example: She was a proponent of the new teaching method.
exampleCn: 她是新教学法的支持者。

enDef: having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something
example: He remained ambivalent about the offer.
exampleCn: 他对这份邀约仍然举棋不定。

enDef: an approach that evaluates theories or beliefs in terms of the success of their practical application
example: Pragmatism guided most of her decisions.
exampleCn: 实用主义指导了她的大多数决策。

enDef: corresponding in size or degree; in proportion
example: Salary will be commensurate with experience.
exampleCn: 薪酬将与经验相称。

enDef: something that is out of place in time, especially something old-fashioned in a modern setting
example: In the digital world, this rule feels like an anachronism.
exampleCn: 在数字世界中，这条规则显得过时。

enDef: conformity to facts; accuracy; truthfulness
example: We must question the veracity of the data.
exampleCn: 我们必须对数据的真实性提出质疑。

enDef: make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse
example: Delay will only exacerbate the problem.
exampleCn: 拖延只会使问题恶化。

enDef: a perfect example of a particular quality or type
example: She is the epitome of academic discipline.
exampleCn: 她是学术自律的典型代表。

enDef: demand or specify a requirement, typically as part of an agreement
example: The contract stipulates a 30-day notice period.
exampleCn: 合同规定了三十天的通知期。

enDef: make an action or process happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly
example: Please expedite the processing of this application.
exampleCn: 请加快这份申请的处理。

enDef: based on observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic
example: The findings were supported by empirical evidence.
exampleCn: 这些发现得到了实证证据的支持。

enDef: showing great care and perseverance
example: Her assiduous research finally yielded results.
exampleCn: 她持之以恒的研究终于取得了成果。
